Three findings that explain the feeling
$23.9M ARR across 905 accounts hasn't been touched this year — that's your whitespace. 62% of those accounts have a High health score, meaning the relationships are strong and the customers are happy. This isn't a retention problem. It's an untapped expansion opportunity sitting right inside the existing book, ready to be worked.
48% of the active book has only Work Management. 1,381 customers chose monday.com, renewed, and never heard about a second product. CRM is at 11% penetration. Dev is at 1.6%. AI is at 4.4%. Every one of these is an open door.
1,191 accounts have AI credits they're barely using — with no AI product. These customers already have AI in their plan and aren't using it. That's the warmest possible opener: show them what they're leaving behind, then introduce the product.
63 accounts are the immediate priority list. Active, $50K+ ARR, High health, WM-only, zero activity this year. Combined ARR: $4.65M. These are the first calls that should happen. Every rep has some.

Expansion opportunity tiers
Immediate action plan
This week — AI credits blitz. Pull every account with AI credits available and usage below 10%. Lead every call this week with the number: "You're using X% of your AI credits. Here's what companies in your sector are doing with the rest." No pitch. Just data. It opens the door to the AI product conversation naturally.
This month — WM-only high-health hitlist. Every rep gets a named list of their WM-only, High-health accounts. Cap it at 15 per rep to start. Build a 60-day expansion plan for each. Multi-product rate doesn't move without a managed list — it moves when reps have a clear target.
30-day deadline — triage the zero-activity accounts. 905 active accounts with ARR have had no commercial touch this year. Assign each rep a deadline: categorize every account in their book as active-and-engaged, active-but-dormant (add to expansion list), or lapsed (start a re-engagement sequence). Don't let them keep hiding in the count.
Focused coaching — Shane Carter, Annika Cannon, Colby Ranck. Shane has the cleanest book in the org but 49.6% zero-activity and 78.8% WM-only. Annika and Colby both exceed 50% zero-activity. These aren't performance problems — they're motion problems. One structured multi-product coaching sprint each would have outsized impact.
Reps running the right motion — peer coaching models
